req.title: Create medication request (audit)
req.statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a prescriber can create a MedicationRequest and an AuditEvent is emitted (positive/EP), (2) a CII controlled substance without schedule verification is rejected with 403 (negative/NEG+DT), (3) a boundary case at exactly the Schedule II threshold tests the transition from non-controlled to controlled-substance workflow (boundary/BVA+STC). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own data, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_create_non_controlled_medication_request_emits_audit
preconditions: Authenticated prescriber token available via <prescriber_token> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Target medication is non-controlled (Schedule V or unscheduled)
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with valid non-controlled medication payload
Assert HTTP 201 and resource ID in response
GET /AuditEvent?entity=<resource_id> to retrieve emitted audit record
expected: HTTP 201 with MedicationRequest resource containing an id
AuditEvent entry exists referencing the created MedicationRequest id
Pharmacy notification flag set in response or side-channel
trace: R-HLT-083: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_non_controlled_medication_request_emits_audit(
    prescriber_token, base_url, cleanup_resource
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {prescriber_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049502", "display": "Amoxicillin 500mg"}]
        },
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-1"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest",
                         json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    resource_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_resource(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest/{resource_id}", headers)

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"MedicationRequest/{resource_id}"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in entries]
    assert "C" in actions

tests.name: test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated prescriber token available via <prescriber_token> fixture
Token does NOT carry schedule-verification claim for CII substances
Target medication is Schedule II (e.g., oxycodone)
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with a Schedule II controlled substance payload
Omit or exclude the schedule-verification header/claim
expected: Response body contains error code indicating missing schedule verification
AuditEvent emitted recording the failed/denied attempt
trace: R-HLT-083: 403 without schedule verification for CII
R-HLT-083: AuditEvent emitted for every operation

tests.name: test_create_cii_medication_request_with_schedule_verification_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated prescriber token available via <prescriber_token_with_schedule> fixture
Token carries valid schedule-verification claim for CII
Target medication is exactly Schedule II (boundary between non-restricted and CII path)
actions: POST /MedicationRequest with Schedule II payload and valid schedule-verification claim
Assert HTTP 201 is returned (boundary: just inside the verified threshold)
GET /AuditEvent for the new resource to confirm audit trail
expected: AuditEvent with action 'C' and outcome '0' (success) references the resource
Response or downstream signal confirms pharmacy notification
trace: R-HLT-083: schedule verification required for CII — passing this boundary case confirms the exact threshold
R-HLT-083: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
